title: tax — published macros
description: The tax module publishes ZERO framework macros. Every consumer touches the tax surface via injected services (TaxCalculator, TaxProviderManager) or the invoice-line listener path. Middleware attaches audit-log context (owned by audit module) — tax consumes it via injection.
rationale: Tax is a CALCULATION ENGINE. The consumer contract is: create an invoice_line → the listener triggers CalculateTaxForInvoiceLineJob → the module persists a tax_calculations snapshot. There is no request-level state that needs to be surfaced as a Request macro (contrast with facility::activeFacility which caches a per-request model).
Adding a tax-specific macro (e.g. Request::activeTaxCalculation) would signal a design smell — tax calculations are per-line-item snapshot artefacts, not persistent per-request state.
The customer's resolved TaxJurisdiction IS potentially request-scoped (for a cart preview page), but it's owned by TaxAddressResolver and consumed via injection — not a macro.
